description: A designed ICE detainee 'rogues-gallery' card in the same standardized template as other ICE arrest graphics. The right side is a desaturated black-and-white close-up portrait of a Hispanic man (Jose Roberto Rodriguez-Urbina) wearing a dark knit beanie, looking directly at the camera, photographed against what appears to be a posted-notice wall in a detention setting. The left side is a dark overlay with typography: a large light-blue/cyan serif all-caps headline 'ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL', a white subhead '1/22/2025 | New York, NY', the detainee's name 'Jose Roberto Rodriguez-Urbina' in bold white sans-serif, then a 'CRIME:' label with a single bullet 'Overstaying terms of Admission to the U.S.'. The DHS/ICE seal and 'ICE' wordmark appear at lower left. A red diagonal corner banner reads 'NEW YORK FIELD OFFICE'. Although the accompanying tweet alleges MS-13 gang membership and extortion charges in El Salvador, the on-image listed crime is only visa overstay. The poster uses mugshot-style framing, darkened portrait and official seals as a propaganda technique to publicize arrests and frame removable migrants as criminals. Target audience: general public and enforcement supporters on social media.
notable_text: ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL; 1/22/2025 | New York, NY; Jose Roberto Rodriguez-Urbina; CRIME: Overstaying terms of Admission to the U.S.; ICE; NEW YORK FIELD OFFICE
